terms.definition: The number of distinct category levels in a feature; high cardinality pushes toward frequency, target, hash, or bucketing strategies instead of full one-hot expansion.
sklearn helper that applies different transformers to different column subsets in one fit/transform call—standard way to compose categorical encoders with numeric scalers in a pipeline.
Replacing a category with how often it appeared in the training set (count or proportion); compresses high-cardinality columns into one numeric feature per field.
Mapping categories to a fixed number of hash buckets via FeatureHasher—useful when vocabulary size is unbounded or memory for one-hot is prohibitive.
Collapsing infrequent category levels into a shared other bucket (often top-K by training frequency) before one-hot or target encoding to stabilize sparse groups.
Library support (e.g. LightGBM, CatBoost) for categorical columns without manual one-hot expansion; split finders treat levels as discrete rather than arbitrary integers.
